name: RENAL AGENESIS
definition: failure of ureteral bud to form rare congenital abnormality of the mesonephric (Wolffian) duct = TRIAD of (1) POTTER SYNDROME hypoplasia of lungs, bowing of legs, broad hands, loose skin, growth retardation
discriminating_features: visualization of single kidney (DDx: additional kidney in ectopic location)

general: visualization of single kidney (DDx: additional kidney in ectopic location)
bilateral absence of kidneys, ureters, renal arteries (after 12 weeks)
inability to visualize renal arteries by color duplex
inability to visualize urine in fetal bladder (after 13 weeks) ← bladder agenesis /
flattened discoid shape of adrenals ← absence of pressure by kidney
bell-shaped thorax in mid to late 3rd trimester ← pulmonary hypoplasia
compression deformities of extremities → clubfoot, flexion contractures, joint dislocations

clinical.Prognosis: stillbirths (24-38%); invariably fatal in the first days of life ← pulmonary
